codeSystems.name: CRAddressCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Address
codeSystems.description: Address codes containing placeholder values that must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the administrative geography of the country or region. The recommended approach uses text-type data elements combined with dependent dropdown lists, aligning with the CanReg5 convention where the address variable is two characters in length encoding a combination of two administrative levels.

codeSystems.name: CRBasisDiagnosisCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Basis diagnosis
codeSystems.description: Basis of diagnosis codes aligned with the CanReg5 basis dictionary. Codes 0-4 represent non-microscopically confirmed diagnoses (death certificate only, clinical, clinical investigation/ultrasound, surgery/autopsy, laboratory test). Codes 5-8 represent microscopically confirmed diagnoses (cytology, histology of metastasis, histology of primary, autopsy with histology). Code 9 represents unknown basis. Used in the Basis data quality check.

codeSystems.name: CRBehaviourCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Behaviour
codeSystems.description: Tumour behaviour codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 behaviour dictionary. Used in the Tumor stage to classify the biological behaviour of the neoplasm. These codes are referenced in data quality checks including the Topography-Behaviour and Sex-Morphology checks. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards.

codeSystems.name: CRGradeCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Grade
codeSystems.description: Tumour grade and differentiation codes aligned with the CanReg5 ICD-O-3.2 grade dictionary. Codes 1-4 represent histological grade (well differentiated to undifferentiated/anaplastic). Codes 5-8 are reserved for lymphomas and leukaemias (T-cell, B-cell, Null cell, NK cell). Code 9 represents unknown/not stated. Grade is mandatory only when behaviour is malignant (3). These codes are used in the Grade data quality check.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphology4CS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology4
codeSystems.description: ICD-O-3.2 morphology codes (first four digits) mapped from the CanReg5 morphology dictionary (morphology4.tsv). These codes represent the histological type of the neoplasm. Since the field is configured as a closed option set, validity of morphology values is guaranteed by the data entry interface. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards and used in multiple data quality checks.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphologyFamilyCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology Family
codeSystems.description: Morphological family grouping codes derived from the CanReg5 MorphFam.txt lookup file. Each morphology code is assigned to a morphological family, which is used in the Sex-Morphology check (to identify rare sex-morphology combinations) and the Topography-Morphology check (to evaluate morphology-topography combinations against MUST and MUST-NOT lookup tables). Code 99 (N/A) indicates the family is accepted with any topography.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphologyGroupCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology Group
codeSystems.description: Morphology group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. Groups are derived from morphology codes using the mapping logic defined in the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester class. Groups 8-14 represent haematopoietic/lymphoid tissue subtypes (systemic groups). Group 0 indicates an invalid morphology, and group 17 indicates an unspecified morphology.

codeSystems.name: CRMorphologyResultCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Morphology result
codeSystems.description: Intermediate morphology evaluation result codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester check. When the morphology comparison between two tumours is inconclusive (undecided), the check proceeds to topography evaluation. 'Invalid' indicates one of the morphology groups is invalid (0). 'Go to topography' indicates the morphology evaluation was undecided and topography comparison is needed.

codeSystems.name: CRMultiplePrimaryTestCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Multiple primary test
codeSystems.description: Result codes for the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. This check determines whether two or more tumours registered for the same patient represent duplicate registrations of the same tumour or distinct multiple primary tumours, based on the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester logic.

codeSystems.name: CRPlaceholderSourceTypeCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Placeholder: Source type
codeSystems.description: Placeholder source type codes for the Source stage. These values must be customised prior to implementation to reflect the source types relevant to the local registry context (e.g. hospital records, pathology laboratory reports, death certificates).

codeSystems.name: CRSexCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Sex
codeSystems.description: Sex codes aligned with the CanReg5 sex dictionary, using numeric codes. These codes are referenced in multiple data quality checks throughout the program, including the Sex-Topography and Sex-Morphology checks. Any modification to these codes or substitution with a differently coded option set would break the logic of those checks.

codeSystems.name: CRSiteCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Site
codeSystems.description: Anatomical site codes used to filter topography values in a dependent dropdown list. When a site is selected, only the topography codes valid for the chosen site are presented for selection, simplifying data entry for registry staff.

codeSystems.name: CRTopographyCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Topography
codeSystems.description: ICD-O-3 topography codes mapped from the CanReg5 topography dictionary (topography.tsv). These codes represent the anatomical site of the tumour origin. The topography field is implemented as a dependent dropdown list filtered by the selected Site. Codes must not be modified as they are mapped directly to CanReg5 standards and used in multiple data quality checks.

codeSystems.name: CRTopographyGroupCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Topography Group
codeSystems.description: Topography group codes used in the Multiple Primary Tester data quality check. Groups are derived from topography codes using the mapping logic defined in the CanReg5 DefaultMultiplePrimaryTester class. Group 80 indicates an unspecified topography. These groups determine whether tumours at different sites should be considered duplicate or multiple primaries.

codeSystems.name: CRTopographyMorphologyKeyCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Topography Morphology key
codeSystems.description: Keys used in the Topography-Morphology data quality check to determine how each morphological family relates to topography. NA (*): the family is accepted with any topography. Plus (+): the family has a restricted set of expected topography sites checked against the MUST list. Minus (-): the family has a restricted set of prohibited topography sites checked against the MUST-NOT list.

codeSystems.name: CRTumorNumberCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Tumor number
codeSystems.description: Tumor number codes used as a unique identifier for each tumor within the registry. Combined with the Patient ID, the Tumor Number constitutes the Tumor ID (the composite identifier that uniquely identifies a tumor record). A validation mechanism prevents the same tumor number from being assigned to more than one tumor belonging to the same patient.

codeSystems.name: CRVitalStatusCS
codeSystems.title: CR - Vital status
codeSystems.description: Vital status codes used in the Follow-up stage to record the current status of the registered patient. When the status is 'Died', the date of death must also be recorded.

codeSystems.name: CREncounterTypeCS
codeSystems.title: Cancer Registry Encounter Types
codeSystems.description: Encounter types representing the three repeatable program stages of the DHIS2 Cancer Registry tracker program.

codeSystems.name: CRObservationCodesCS
codeSystems.title: Cancer Registry Observation Codes
codeSystems.description: Code system defining observation type codes for cancer registry observations. Used where no standard LOINC code exists for cancer-registry-specific concepts.

codeSystems.name: Dhis2DataElementsCS
codeSystems.title: DHIS2 Data Elements
codeSystems.description: Code system for 47 unique DHIS2 data elements from all selected programs

codeSystems.name: Dhis2TrackedEntityAttributesCS
codeSystems.title: DHIS2 Tracked Entity Attributes
codeSystems.description: Code system for 5 unique DHIS2 tracked entity attributes
